episode_summary: The team discuss ancient and unusual food and drink, surprising mortality statistics and name coincidences, and the early, eye-watering history of contact lenses. Topics range from a wine-filled proto-sandwich to deaths attributed to acne, notable non-Albert Einsteins, and contact lenses that once cost as much as a car.

facts.fact: Yeah, I found out this week that the first ever sandwich that we know about contained wine.
It's in 2003, three people in Mexico died of acne.
It's all the other notable Einstein's out there.
The fact is, the first contact lenses cost as much as a car.
